title: Complete Documentation of workflow:plan, workflow:lite-plan, and issue:plan Implementations
summary: Generate comprehensive technical documentation for three workflow implementations covering Slash Command structure, Skill integration, JSON Schema definitions, CCW server interaction, and agent development patterns
approach: Multi-layer documentation strategy: (1) Command layer: analyze each workflow's execution model and orchestration, (2) CLI system layer: document Slash command registration and parameter handling, (3) Agent layer: map agent orchestration patterns and responsibilities, (4) Schema layer: document JSON schema specifications and validation, (5) Server layer: document CCW server integration and protocols, (6) Skill layer: document skill definition and lifecycle, (7) Reference layer: provide code references and cross-links

tasks.title: Document workflow:plan implementation (5-phase orchestration)
tasks.description: Analyze and document the complete 5-phase workflow:plan orchestration: Phase 1 Session Discovery, Phase 2 Context Gathering, Phase 3 Conflict Resolution, Phase 4 Task Generation, Phase 5 Summary. Include phase descriptions, decision trees, data flow, and examples.
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Document includes: (1) Phase 1 detailed steps with SlashCommand orchestration, (2) Phase 2 context gathering with 3-track strategy, (3) Phase 3 conditional conflict resolution logic, (4) Phase 4 task generation with action-planning-agent, (5) Phase 5 summary and recommendations, (6) Complete execution flow diagram, (7) At least 3 file:line references to source code

tasks.title: Document workflow:lite-plan implementation (lightweight 5-phase)
tasks.description: Analyze and document the lightweight 5-phase workflow:lite-plan: Phase 1 Task Analysis with complexity assessment, Phase 2 Multi-angle Clarification, Phase 3 Planning with Direct Claude vs cli-lite-planning-agent decision, Phase 4 User Confirmation, Phase 5 In-memory execution handoff.
tasks.modification_points: .claude/commands/workflow/lite-plan.md, complexity assessment logic, ANGLE_PRESETS configuration, cli-lite-planning-agent invocation
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Document includes: (1) Complexity assessment metrics (Low/Medium/High), (2) Exploration angle selection logic with ANGLE_PRESETS, (3) AskUserQuestion integration for clarification, (4) Direct Claude vs agent planning decision criteria, (5) In-memory context structure for handoff, (6) Comparison matrix with workflow:plan, (7) At least 3 file:line references

tasks.title: Document issue:plan implementation (closed-loop planning)
tasks.description: Analyze and document issue:plan's batch processing, semantic grouping, failure-aware replanning, and solution generation. Include single-solution auto-bind and multi-solution user selection patterns.
tasks.modification_points: .claude/commands/issue/plan.md, issue-plan-agent orchestration, failure analysis from issue.feedback, solution binding logic
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Document includes: (1) Batch processing (1-3 issues) strategy, (2) Semantic grouping algorithm, (3) Failure history analysis from issue.feedback, (4) ACE semantic search exploration workflow, (5) Solution generation schema, (6) Auto-bind vs user selection logic, (7) GitHub issue commenting integration, (8) At least 4 file:line references

tasks.title: Document Slash Command registration and lifecycle
tasks.description: Document the complete Slash Command system: YAML frontmatter parsing (name, description, allowed-tools), command discovery mechanism, argument parsing, variable substitution, command routing, and execution lifecycle.
tasks.modification_points: .claude/commands/ directory structure, slash command parser, command registry, argument schema validation
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Document includes: (1) YAML frontmatter fields and validation, (2) Command discovery algorithm, (3) Argument parsing from inline arguments vs --flags, (4) Variable substitution rules (${VAR}, @file patterns), (5) Command routing logic, (6) Execution context building, (7) Error handling for missing commands/args, (8) At least 5 file:line references to command implementations

tasks.title: Document agent orchestration patterns and Task tool invocation
tasks.description: Document how agents are orchestrated through Task tool invocations: synchronous vs background execution, task context building, result collection, parallel execution coordination, and error recovery.
tasks.modification_points: .claude/agents/ structure, Task tool parameters (subagent_type, run_in_background, prompt, max_turns), agent result parsing, task batching strategies
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Document includes: (1) Five agent types and their responsibilities, (2) Synchronous (run_in_background=false) vs async execution patterns, (3) Context loading in agent prompts, (4) Parallel execution coordination (max tasks, queuing), (5) Result parsing and error handling, (6) Agent resume capability (agent_id), (7) Batch processing limits and strategies, (8) At least 5 file:line references to agent implementations

tasks.title: Document JSON Schema specifications and validation
tasks.description: Document all JSON schemas used by workflows: plan-json-schema.json, fix-plan-json-schema.json, solution-schema.json, and others. Include validation rules, field requirements, optional fields, enums, and pattern matching.
tasks.modification_points: .claude/workflows/cli-templates/schemas/*.json, schema validation logic, type definitions, optional vs required fields
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Document includes: (1) plan-json-schema.json with all required/optional fields, (2) fix-plan-json-schema.json with severity and risk_level enums, (3) solution-schema.json with task structure (T1-T5 phases), (4) task template schema with acceptance_criteria quantification rules, (5) Validation error examples and error messages, (6) Schema evolution and versioning strategy, (7) At least 3 schema files with complete field documentation, (8) Examples of valid/invalid JSON instances

tasks.title: Document CCW server integration and communication protocols
tasks.description: Document how CLI tools communicate with CCW server: streaming execution, hook callbacks, dashboard notifications, WebSocket messaging, state synchronization, and recovery mechanisms.
tasks.modification_points: ccw/ directory implementation, CLI output streaming (CLI_EXECUTION_STARTED, CLI_OUTPUT, CLI_EXECUTION_COMPLETED), hook callback system, WebSocket protocol, dashboard state updates
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Document includes: (1) Streaming execution events (START, OUTPUT, COMPLETED), (2) Hook callback structure and registration, (3) Dashboard notification mechanism, (4) WebSocket message format and types, (5) State synchronization protocol, (6) Recovery from connection loss, (7) Error propagation from CLI to server, (8) At least 5 server implementation file:line references

tasks.title: Document Skill definition structure and lifecycle
tasks.description: Document the Skill system: SKILL.md frontmatter (name, description, phases), skill discovery and registration, sequential vs autonomous execution modes, file-based vs memory-based context strategies, and progressive loading.
tasks.modification_points: .claude/skills/ structure, SKILL.md parser, skill registry, execution mode selection, context caching, progressive artifact loading
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Document includes: (1) SKILL.md frontmatter structure and fields, (2) Sequential vs autonomous execution decision logic, (3) File-based context loading with progressive increments, (4) Memory-based context strategies, (5) Skill discovery and registration mechanism, (6) Execution lifecycle (init, execute, cleanup), (7) Error handling and skill recovery, (8) At least 4 skill implementations with file:line references

tasks.title: Document context-search-agent (3-track discovery strategy)
tasks.description: Analyze context-search-agent: (1) Bash track with command execution, (2) Semantic track with ACE search, (3) Web research track with Exa. Document relevance scoring, deduplication, and conflict detection.
tasks.modification_points: .claude/agents/context-search-agent.md, 3-track execution, relevance scoring formula, conflict detection algorithm, risk assessment
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Document includes: (1) Three discovery tracks with invocation conditions, (2) Relevance scoring formula (multi-factor weighting), (3) Deduplication logic, (4) Conflict detection between tracks, (5) Risk assessment scoring, (6) File organization pattern detection, (7) Integration point suggestions with file:line references, (8) Example execution with all three tracks

tasks.title: Document action-planning-agent (task generation and IMPL_PLAN.md)
tasks.description: Analyze action-planning-agent: task decomposition, execution group assignment, IMPL_PLAN.md template generation, cross-artifact consistency checking, and quality validation.
tasks.modification_points: .claude/agents/action-planning-agent.md, task generation logic, IMPL_PLAN.md template, execution group strategies, quality criteria validation
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Document includes: (1) Task decomposition algorithm and heuristics, (2) Execution group assignment strategies (sequential, parallel, parallel-dependents), (3) IMPL_PLAN.md template structure and sections, (4) Dependency resolution and cycle detection, (5) Quality criteria quantification rules, (6) Cross-artifact consistency checking, (7) Reference to action-plan-verify command integration, (8) Example task generation output with 5+ tasks

execution_order.description: Document three core workflows first as foundation
Document agent subsystems last (dependencies on T4, T5)

exit_conditions.success: All 10 documentation tasks completed with evidence
exit_conditions.failure: Task blocked or cannot generate required documentation

verification_steps: (1) All 10 tasks completed with documentation output
(5) Execution flow diagrams for all three workflows
(6) Integration point documentation with code examples

design_decisions.decision: Organize by integration layers (command, CLI, agent, schema, server, skill)
design_decisions.rationale: Enables clear understanding of how components interact across layers
design_decisions.tradeoff: May require some cross-referencing between documents

design_decisions.decision: Provide file:line references for all major components
design_decisions.rationale: Allows developers to quickly locate implementation details
design_decisions.tradeoff: Requires validating references don't change during documentation

design_decisions.decision: Include examples and diagrams for each workflow
design_decisions.rationale: Improves clarity and provides templates for new workflow development
